_meta.description: Translation table: the game gear knob → VST parameter. Curated around Kilohearts Essentials (free) — consistent param names across the entire bundle.

<rs_knob_name>.param: <exact VST param name as shown in the in-app editor>
<rs_knob_name>.scale: <float, multiply RS value by this. 0.01 for 0-100 → 0-1; 0.0005 for ms 0-2000; 1.0 for direct dB / semitones>
<rs_knob_name>.invert: <optional bool, replaces v with (1-v) after scale>

kilohearts_param_naming_convention._note: KHS modules use consistent param names across the entire Essentials bundle. Common params you'll see in every module:
kilohearts_param_naming_convention.mix: Dry/Wet (0-1) — some older docs show 'Mix'; the in-app editor will show the real name

notes: If a param name doesn't match, open the in-app VST editor for that gear, copy the exact label from a slider, and paste here. The lookup is case-insensitive but otherwise exact.
VST name stems below are lowercased to match the case-insensitive lookup. Plugin files on disk are 'kHs Chorus.vst3' etc. — the matcher strips '.vst3' and lowercases automatically.

EQ pedals (EQ5/EQ8/BassEQ8): knobs are absolute frequencies (e.g. '5700': 5 = +5 dB at 5700 Hz). Verified band layouts (from inspect_rs_knobs.py against a real library): EQ5 = 63/250/750/2200/5700 Hz; EQ8 = 50/100/200/400/800/1600/3200/6400 Hz; BassEQ8 = 30/75/185/460/1100/2700/6800/16000 Hz. kHs 3-Band EQ absorbs three of those — the entries below pick the most musical bands per pedal. The other RS frequencies are silently dropped (lossy). For lossless mapping, switch the gear to Voxengo Marvel GEQ (16-band) and add a per-pedal entry under 'voxengo marvel geq'.

Rack_StudioEQ (4-band parametric: Bass/LoMid/HiMid/Treble with Q + Freq knobs) is the highest-coverage EQ in the library. Currently lossy-mapped to kHs 3-Band (gain bands only — Q/Freq dropped). For full fidelity switch the gear to TDR Nova (4-band parametric, free) and add a 'tdr nova' entry mapping <Band>+<BandFreq>+<BandQ> per band.

Melda free (MFreeFXBundle): MTremolo, MVibrato, MFlanger, MPhaser, MRingModulator, MEqualizer, MCompressor are now PRIMARY for those categories alongside the existing KHS entries. Melda paramId convention is consistent across the family — verifying one editor (e.g. MTremolo) validates all 7 Melda mappings.

_static block: a VST entry may include `"_static": {"<vst_param_name>": <normalized_value>, ...}` to apply UNCONDITIONAL params regardless of RS knobs. Used to pin curated character knobs (e.g. kHs Distortion Type + Dynamics) so every fuzz pedal sounds like a fuzz, every overdrive sounds like an overdrive. Values are normalized [0,1]. For stepped/enum params, verify the dropdown order via `await window.rbSweepParam(slotId, paramName)` from DevTools — names + numeric ranges differ across plugins.

kHs Distortion subtype convention (user-curated, verified via rbSweepParam): the `Type` param has 6 discrete modes — Overdrive (0.0), Saturate (0.2), Foldback (0.4), Sine (0.6), Hard Clip (0.8), Quantize (1.0). Fuzz pedals → Type=0.2 (Saturate); overdrive pedals → Type=0.0 (Overdrive); distortion pedals → Type=0.8 (Hard Clip). All three pin Dynamics=1.0 so the pedal's response tracks the player's pick attack.
_meta.stem_alias_note: Bundled VST files were renamed for the no-copyright UI (e.g. EQ8->GE-8, StudioComp->HZX, MultiPitch->'Multi Pitch'). Added lowercased real-filename-stem aliases so _build_params_for_piece resolves the same param block. 2 gears (Bass_Pedal_BassWah, Bass_Pedal_BassSubOctave) still lack an authored bundled mapping.

dualrect._hint: Silla Boogie Duo Rectifier = the FULL 3-channel Dual Rectifier panel. The 5 RS knobs map 1:1 onto the RED channel (the signature high-gain voice); the channel is pinned to RED in MODERN mode with a BOLD (tight silicon) rectifier and Cab Sim via _static. Green/Orange channels sit at their defaults and stay editable by hand.

Mid._hint: RS Mid -> Red channel Mid; low Mid deepens the signature Recto scoop (more so in Modern).

box ac30._hint: BOX AC30 = the FULL Vox AC30C2 panel (modelled from Vox_ac30c2.pdf). The AC30 has no Mid or Bright pot, so those RS knobs are mapped to REAL circuit behaviour: Mid -> Normal-channel Volume with the input jumpered to Both (the classic AC30 channel-jumper fills the Vox mid-scoop), and Bright -> the Top Boost brilliance bright-cap. Gain/Treble/Bass/Pres map straight onto the panel.

Gain._hint: RS Gain drives the Top-Boost (Brilliant) channel Volume — the AC30C2's real gain control. Output loudness is held flat across the range by the model's makeup. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

Treble._hint: RS Treble maps to the Top-Boost tone-stack Treble (VR3 A1M).

Bass._hint: RS Bass maps to the Top-Boost tone-stack Bass (VR4 A1M).

Mid._hint: The AC30 has no Mid pot. With the input jumpered to Both (_static Input=0.5), RS Mid turns up the Normal channel — its mid-forward voicing fills the Top Boost mid-scoop, exactly like jumpering the two channels on a real AC30. Loudness stays ~flat (model compensates).

Pres._hint: Pres is the inverse of the AC30 Tone Cut (VR9 + C80 4n7): higher RS Pres -> less cut -> more EL84/OT bite. Cab Sim is a fallback speaker voice until feedBack routes an external cabinet/IR.

Bright._hint: RS Bright (0/1) sets the Top Boost brilliance bright-cap (preamp treble sparkle, distinct from the power-amp Tone Cut so it doesn't fight Pres).

_static._note: Controls the game songs don't set, pinned to the reference: Input jumpered to Both so RS Mid (-> Normal Vol) can blend the Normal channel in, reverb level off (tone centred), tremolo off (speed parked), Master at the calibrated ~0.40 RMS loudness reference, fallback Cab Sim on until the host bypasses it when an external cabinet/IR is present.

bender deluxe._hint: BENDER DELUXE = the FULL Fender '57 Deluxe 5E3 panel (Tone, Inst Vol, Mic Vol only). On a 5E3 the VOLUME is the gain and there is one Tone control; the famous trick is jumpering the Mic channel for body. So RS Gain -> Inst Vol, Treble -> Tone, Mid -> Mic Vol (jumper). Bright picks the Instrument bright/normal input; Bass + Pres have no 5E3 pot so they drive hidden shelves.

Gain._hint: On a 5E3 the Instrument Volume IS the gain — it cleans up / breaks up as you turn it. RS Gain drives it; loudness is held flat by the makeup. RS 0 maps to a low-but-audible real pot position; the curve is trimmed so the Deluxe does not break up too early.

Treble._hint: RS Treble maps to the single tweed Tone control (treble bleed: up = bright, down = dark).

Mid._hint: The 5E3 has no Mid pot. RS Mid turns up the jumpered Mic channel — its darker, low-mid-forward voice fills body underneath the Instrument channel (the classic 5E3 jumper).

Bright._hint: RS Bright picks the Instrument bright input 1 (bright cap) vs the normal/darker input 2.

Bass._hint: The 5E3 has no Bass pot; RS Bass drives a hidden low shelf (tweed thump / tightening).

Pres._hint: The 5E3 has no presence pot; RS Pres drives a hidden power-amp top lift.

plexi._hint: Marsten Plexi = the FULL Marshall 1959 Super Lead panel. The RS amp exposes the two Loudness pots directly (gain_proxy_knobs = Loudness1/Loudness2), NOT a generic Gain — so they map 1:1: Loudness1 -> HIGH TREBLE/bright channel (Loudness I), Loudness2 -> NORMAL channel (Loudness II). The Loudness pots ARE the gain (non-master amp), so cranking them drives the EL34 grind. Bass/Mid/Treble -> Marshall tone stack (33K slope, 220pF treble cap), Pres -> Presence. Aliases (Middle/Presence/HighTreble/Normal/Gain) are tolerated for whatever the tone actually carries; absent keys are ignored.

Loudness1._hint: RS Loudness1 -> Loudness I (HIGH TREBLE / bright channel). The plexi breaks up via loudness; higher = more EL34 power-amp grind. Held ~flat by the makeup. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

Loudness2._hint: RS Loudness2 -> Loudness II (NORMAL channel). Up = the fuller jumpered plexi voice. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

Bass._hint: RS Bass -> Marshall tone-stack bass (1M).

Mid._hint: RS Mid -> Marshall tone-stack middle (22K).

Middle._hint: alias: some tones spell the mid knob 'Middle'.

Treble._hint: RS Treble -> Marshall tone-stack treble (220K, 220pF) + bright-channel sparkle.

Pres._hint: RS Pres -> Presence (power-amp NFB) + upper speaker bite.

Gain._hint: fallback: if a tone exposes a generic Gain instead of Loudness1, drive Loudness I. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

_static._note: Input pinned to BOTH (jumpered) so both RS Loudness pots feed the amp — the classic fat plexi tone. Cab Sim stays on until the host bypasses it for an external cabinet/IR. Click the input cable on the face to switch to Bright-only / Normal-only.

citrusjimmybean._hint: Citrus Jimmy Bean = the Orange Jimmy Bean JB150 (1975 solid-state twin-channel + tremolo + sustain). RS Gain -> SUSTAIN (solid-state sustain/compressor), Bass/Treble -> Baxandall EQ. _static pins Volume, tremolo OFF, CH1, Bright off.

Gain._hint: RS Gain -> SUSTAIN (solid-state compression/soft-clip). Loudness held ~flat by the makeup. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

Bass._hint: RS Bass -> Baxandall bass.

Treble._hint: RS Treble -> Baxandall treble.

_static._note: Volume at noon, tremolo OFF (Depth 0), CH1, Bright off. Cab Sim stays on until the host bypasses it for an external cabinet/IR. Dial Speed/Depth/Channel/Bright on the face by hand.

multiversal610._hint: Multiversal 610 = the UA 610-A tube DI/preamp. RS Gain -> Gain (V1 drive); Bass -> Low EQ; Treble -> High EQ. The real 610 has NO mid control, so RS Mid/MidFreq are dropped. _static pins Level, Lo gain, Line input.

Gain._hint: RS Gain 0-100 -> V1 12AX7 drive 0..1. RS 0 now maps to a low-but-audible real pot position instead of muting the amp.

Bass._hint: RS Bass 0-100 (50=flat) -> LF stepped shelf -6..+6 dB.

Treble._hint: RS Treble 0-100 (50=flat) -> HF stepped shelf -6..+6 dB.

_static._note: Output at the loudness reference, Lo gain, Line (instrument DI). Tweak on the face by hand.

sinampbassdriver._hint: SinAmp Bass Driver = the Tech 21 SansAmp BDDI. RS Gain -> Drive; Bass/Treble -> active tone; Blend -> Blend; Pres -> Presence. _static pins Mid flat, Level, shifts off.

Gain._hint: RS Gain 0-100 -> DRIVE into the zener clippers 0..1.

Bass._hint: RS Bass 0-100 (50=flat) -> BASS +/-12 dB.

Treble._hint: RS Treble 0-100 (50=flat) -> TREBLE +/-12 dB.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 -> dry-buffer vs amp-emulation mix.

Pres._hint: RS Pres 0-100 -> PRESENCE pre-clip HF boost.

_static._note: Mid flat, Level at reference, shifts off. Tweak on the face.

meve1073._hint: Meve 1073 = the Neve 1073 console pre/EQ as a DI. RS Gain -> Gain; Bass -> Low; Mid -> Mid (PRESENCE); Treble -> High. _static pins the freq selectors, HPF off and Output.

Gain._hint: RS Gain 0-100 -> EK20033 sensitivity / class-A drive 0..1.

Bass._hint: RS Bass 0-100 (50=flat) -> LOW shelf +/-16 dB.

Mid._hint: RS Mid 0-100 (50=flat) -> PRESENCE peak +/-18 dB.

Treble._hint: RS Treble 0-100 (50=flat) -> HIGH shelf +/-16 dB @ 12 kHz.

_static._note: LOW @ 110 Hz, PRESENCE @ 1.6 kHz, HPF off, Output at reference. Pick freqs/HPF on the face.
